title: Petrosedum × elaverinum
taxonomy: Petrosedum × elaverinum is classified as a succulent plant, belonging to the family Crassulaceae. It is a hybrid of Petrosedum araxaense and Petrosedum rupestre. Petrosedum × elaverinum is native to the Iberian Peninsula, where it grows in rocky areas. This plant is known for its small, white flowers and distinctive rosettes of fleshy leaves. Petrosedum × elaverinum is a popular choice among succulent collectors due to its unique appearance and low-maintenance requirements.
